name: Trace POP
description: The Trace POP reads a TOP image and traces it, generating line strips around areas exceeding a certain brightness threshold. You can control the threshold using the Threshold parameter, and compare it with the pixel value determined by the Channel menu, by default the brightness. The line strips can be triangulated into triangular surfaces, ore left in its basic (fastest but least useful) form as 2-point lines. The resolution of the resulting line strips or triangle meshes can be reduced using the Resolution Multiplier parameter. You can smooth the line strips using the Smooth Edge Distance parameter which is similar to the snoothing in the [[Line Smooth POP]]. The Extend menu determines is the line strips continue around the border or are separates as open line strips. The Winding will make outer line strips that are counter-clockwise, and line strips enclosed in them to be clockwise. The output is -.5 to .5 in X (P(0)) but it can be re-ranged with the ReRange P parameters. You may need to adjust the Max Num Line Strips parameter or the Max Num Verts parameter (since these numbers need to be pre-determined for the GPU to avoid overflows). See also [[Polygonize POP]], [[Triangulate POP]], [[Extrude POP]]

parameters.name: Input Attribute Scope
parameters.label: Input Attribute Scope
parameters.description: When using a POP with dimension 2 as an input, the attribute scope to sample.

parameters.name: Use Position Attribute
parameters.label: Use Position Attribute
parameters.description: When using a POP with dimension 2 as an input, whether to use the P attribute to position the threshold lines.

parameters.name: TOP
parameters.label: TOP
parameters.description: TOP to sample.

parameters.name: Channel
parameters.label: Channel
parameters.description: When using a TOP input, the color channel to sample.

parameters.name: Resolution Multiplier
parameters.label: Resolution Multiplier
parameters.description: Resolution multiplier to apply to the input.

parameters.name: Threshold
parameters.label: Threshold
parameters.description: The threshold value to determine what is inside the surface and what is outside (empty).

parameters.name: Inside
parameters.label: Inside
parameters.description: Whether values above the threshold or below or equal the threshold are considered inside. For contours, that determines the direction of the segments.

parameters.name: Extend
parameters.label: Extend
parameters.description: How to sample outside the boundaries, which is needed to determine what happens at the boundaries. Zero returns 0, Hold returns the boundary value.

parameters.name: Output
parameters.label: Output
parameters.description: Whether to output a contour, lines or line strips, or a surface made of triangles.

parameters.name: Unique Points
parameters.label: Unique Points
parameters.description: When outputting Lines, whether the points are shared between the lines or unique to each line.

parameters.name: Winding
parameters.label: Winding
parameters.description: When outputting line strips, by default the closed line strips will be oriented counterclockwise around surfaces, and clockwise around holes. Counterclockwise reverses the line strips around holes so they're counterclockwise as well.

parameters.name: Smooth Edge Distance
parameters.label: Smooth Edge Distance
parameters.description: When outputting line strips, smoothes the line strips, averaging neighboring points positions, using the distance value.

parameters.name: ReRange P
parameters.label: ReRange P
parameters.description: Whether to ReRange P.

parameters.name: Map to Low
parameters.label: Map to Low
parameters.description: The low value of the new range in X and Y.

parameters.name: Map to High
parameters.label: Map to High
parameters.description: The high value of the new range in X d Y.

parameters.name: Normal
parameters.label: Normal
parameters.description: Whether to output a Normal (N) attribute.

parameters.name: Normal Direction
parameters.label: Normal Direction
parameters.description: Chooses the normal direction when outputting normals.

parameters.name: Texture Coordinates
parameters.label: Texture Coordinates
parameters.description: Whether to output a point Texture Coordinates attribute (Tex)

parameters.name: Fraction of Max Allocation
parameters.label: Fraction of Max Allocation
parameters.description: This POP doesn't know in advance how much GPU memory it will need, but except in rare cases (very high amount of detail, a lot of variation in neighboring pixels in the source) it doesn't need the maximum allocation. This allows to save GPU memory. If points and primitives are missing or the info popup shows the max numbers are reached, it means the fraction is too low.

parameters.name: Max Num Line Strips
parameters.label: Max Num Line Strips
parameters.description: Optional toggle, saves some GPU memory when set correctly.

parameters.name: Max Num Verts per Line Strip
parameters.label: Max Num Verts per Line Strip
parameters.description: Optional toggle, can help prevent the GPU being stuck in infinite loops, doesn't save memory.
menuLabels: Max Num Verts per Line Strip
Max Num Verts per Line Strip

parameters.name: Copy Topology Info Back to CPU
parameters.label: Copy Topology Info Back to CPU
parameters.description: Copy back the point count info and topology info held on the GPU back to CPU. Causes a stall for discrete GPUs.

_provenance.fact: scraped from the live Trace_POP wiki page
